untranslatable_reason: This is a STOP PLACEMENT rule based on statistical analysis (MAE percentile), not a POSITION SIZING rule. It describes where to set stops relative to entry, not how much capital/risk to allocate per trade. Position sizing (how many shares/contracts to buy) requires knowing the stop distance, but this rule only defines the stop level methodology. To become a sizing rule, it would need to specify: 'Risk X% per trade with stops set at 75th percentile MAE' — which it does not.
